parsed.case_level_notes: Clean general 5-year track packet; all eligibility criteria satisfied.

determinations.rationale: LPR for 6 years with only one 21-day trip, well under the 6-month break threshold.
41 months physical presence exceeds the 30-month requirement for the 5-year track.
9 months in same USCIS district exceeds the 3-month requirement.
English test passed per naturalization test record.
Civics test passed per naturalization test record.
Applicant willing to take oath and attachment to Constitution recorded.
No aggravated felony, no pending charges, no false testimony issues reported.
No missing, inconsistent, or pending facts triggering human review; all requirements clearly met.

parsed.case_level_notes: Conflicting travel evidence and a ~198-day absence require adjudicator review of continuous residence rebuttal and applicant credibility.

determinations.rationale: Objective records show an absence of approximately 198 days (over six months, under one year), which creates a rebuttable presumption of broken continuous residence. Although the applicant has lease, bank, and family ties evidence that may rebut the presumption, the travel table conflicts with passport/CBP records, requiring adjudicator review.
The packet does not provide total physical presence days across the five-year period; only one trip is documented with conflicting durations. Insufficient facts to compute the 30-month threshold.
No facts in the packet address state or USCIS district residence duration.
Packet contains no facts about English proficiency or exceptions.
Packet contains no facts about civics knowledge or exceptions.
No facts about attachment to the Constitution or oath are provided.

The inconsistency between the applicant's travel table/personal statement and objective passport/CBP records raises a potential candor/GMC issue requiring adjudicator review. No other GMC bars discussed.

Inconsistent travel history between the N-400 table, personal statement, and passport/CBP records, plus an absence between six and twelve months, trigger human review for continuous residence rebuttal and possible misrepresentation/GMC concerns.

parsed.case_level_notes: Continuous residence is broken by an absence exceeding one year (Jan 4, 2021 to Feb 10, 2022) with no N-470, employment-abroad, military, or religious-vocation exception. Other eligibility elements are not addressed in the packet.

determinations.rationale: Absence from January 4, 2021 to February 10, 2022 exceeds one year, breaking continuous residence, with no N-470 or other preservation exception in the packet.
Packet does not provide total physical presence days within the statutory period; only one absence is documented.
No information about state or USCIS district residence provided in the packet.
No facts in the packet address English proficiency or exceptions.
No facts in the packet address civics knowledge or exceptions.
Packet contains no information on oath or attachment to the Constitution.
Packet contains no GMC-related facts to evaluate.
Absence exceeding one year with no preservation exception is a benchmark trigger; case should be reviewed and likely denied for broken continuous residence.

parsed.case_level_notes: Applicant fails physical presence under the five-year track per worksheet (24 < 30 months). Other eligibility prongs lack facts in packet.

determinations.rationale: No facts provided regarding continuous residence absences or breaks.

parsed.case_level_notes: Critical evidence (passport, CBP travel history, flight records, travel table) is missing; only an approximate narrative exists. Human review required to obtain exact travel dates before adjudicating continuous residence and physical presence.
